titleStmt.title: SII 23.589: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 589.

summary.p: This is a Telugu inscription dated Śaka 1[5]41, Siddhārthi, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 12, Wednesday corresponding to A.D. 1619, August 11. It was Nija-Śrāvaṇa month. The record states that a certain Chennammagāru a lady of the royal household of Tiruveṅgaḷanātharāja, with a view to secure śāśvata-parama-pada without any more birth in the mundane world, offered endless worship to god Saumyanāthasvāmi at Nelandalūru otherwise known as Nirantarapura, and made a number of gifts to the deity such as a golden crown, conch and discus, golden plates for the hands and feet, a golden kalaśa over the vimāna and made provision for feeding the Śrīvaishṇavas and the Smārtas in their respective feeding houses and also the Paramahamsas and Jōgi-jaṅgas.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . jayābhyudaya śālivāhana śakhavarṣaṁbulu veyayyanūṭi nalubhaivokkaṭi Agunēṁṭi siddhārthisaṁvatsara śrāvaṇa śuddhadvādaśi budhavārāna niraṁtarapurōnagu(gu) pratināmamaina nelaṁdalūra veṁcēsivunna śrī saumyanātha svā
mi veṁgaḷanātharājuvāri nagarivāru cennamagāru tamaku punarāvṛttirahita śāśvata paramapadāvāpti koraku Anaṁtamulugā sēviṁci svāmiki baṁgārāna kirīṭamunu śaṁkhacakrālunnu kaṭihastamunu śrī
pādālunnu divyavimānamunaku kalaśamunnu baṁgārāna śēyiṁci samarpiṁci svāmi tamapara Avasaramani Āragiṁcina prasādaṁ śrīvaiṣṇavulaku rāmānujakūṭamunnu smārthulaku satramunnu paramahaṁsalaku jōgija
ṁgānaku daṁḍigā prasāpāṭu gaṭṭuṭa śēśināru ganuka . dānapālanayōrmadhyē dānāśchrēyōnupālanaṁ . dānātsvargamavāpnōti pālanādacyutaṁ padaṁ .
Digital edition of SII 23.589 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
